chosen_logo.why: uco-c-header__logo-image — official self-hosted SVG at /core-assets/logos/logo-wordmark.svg, alt='Unilever logo - go to home'. Sits in <header> on every fetched page. SVG vector means clean currentColor flip between white-on-blue (cover) and brand-blue-on-paper (content). The signature flowing handwritten 'Unilever' wordmark Wolff Olins designed in 2004.

brand_primary.name: --primary
brand_primary.evidence: Sky blue — appears 26× in CSS, h2/h3 color, button-bg-color → var(--color-brand-sky). Also the visible header gradient base in screenshots. Per the root_vars system this maps to --color-brand-sky.

brand_secondary.name: --accent
brand_secondary.evidence: Sky-dark — `<a>` background rgb(0,77,153) seen across all 6 sampled pages; --button-hover-bg-color resolves to --color-brand-skydark. Used for hover states / link surfaces / deep-blue accents.

ink.name: --ink
ink.evidence: Body text rgb(77,75,70) on every fetched page — Unilever's signature warm-graphite ink, NOT pure black. 'Stone'-named in their root_vars as --color-brand-stone.

paper.name: --surface
paper.evidence: Computed body bg rgb(246,247,240) on every page — soft cream-tinted paper, --color-brand-cloud. Distinctive warm off-white that gives Unilever its editorial-organic feel (NOT a SaaS pure-white).

accents.name: --gold
accents.evidence: Sustainability hero / award accents — from root_vars --color-brand-magma family adjacent. Warm gold pairs with the cream paper and reinforces 'sustainability gravitas' mood.

accents.name: --aquamid
accents.evidence: Listicle keyline / dot color → --color-brand-aquamid. Soft eco-aqua used as separator / row accent across long-form content.

accents.name: --cloudblue
accents.evidence: Light atmospheric tint pairing with sky — used as form-input bg via --color-brand-cloud variant. Soft surface accent for callouts.

accents.name: --white
accents.evidence: Pure white — used as inverse paper on the blue cover (logo flip target) and on dark-hero overlays.

neutrals.name: --rule
neutrals.evidence: Hairline grey — appears 30× in CSS, used for dividers / disabled states / form borders.

neutrals.name: --mid
neutrals.evidence: Mid-grey for muted secondary text — 24× in CSS.

display_font.evidence: h1/h2/h3 computed font-family across all 6 pages: 'UnileverDesire, Century Gothic, AppleGothic, system-ui...' UnileverDesire is the proprietary Wolff Olins-designed face — appears 62× in fonts.frequencies. NOT licensable for standalone slide decks; need fallback discussion in Phase 2.

body_font.evidence: Same fallback chain on body — Unilever uses ONE family across display + body. The chain falls back to Century Gothic (geometric humanist sans) which is the right register fallback if UnileverDesire isn't available.

spacing_radius_shadow.radius_style: split — sharp 0px on most surfaces, full pill (26px nav, 32px CTA half-pill) on interactive controls
spacing_radius_shadow.shadow_style: flat — no box-shadow on top-level chrome anywhere
spacing_radius_shadow.evidence: Computed body / h1 / h2 radius all 0px; nav radius 26px (full pill); CTA radius '0px 32px 32px 0px' (asymmetric half-pill — Unilever's signature shape). Shadow 'none' on every surface.

precedents: Patagonia annual reports — long-form sustainability narrative + warm paper + earthy organic palette
FT Weekend Magazine — editorial register, generous whitespace, warm not stark
old IBM brochures — flowing custom wordmark + corporate confidence + asymmetric layout
aesthetic.voice_register: corporate but humanist; sustainability-led, evidence-heavy; the wordmark and the paper carry the warmth; statements are confident not slick
